evals.name: springboot-java-maven-happy-path
evals.prompt: Setup DynamoDBTargetSystem for my Spring Boot Java app using Maven.
evals.expected_output: A complete Spring Boot @Bean configuration for DynamoDBTargetSystem with Maven dependencies and canonical placeholders.
expectations: Uses @Bean public DynamoDBTargetSystem
Includes flamingock-springboot-integration and flamingock-dynamodb-targetsystem
Includes AWS DynamoDB SDK dependency
Uses YOUR_TARGET_SYSTEM_ID and TODO placeholders
Language is Java

evals.name: standalone-kotlin-gradle-new-client
evals.prompt: I need to configure Flamingock for a standalone Kotlin project with Gradle. I don't have a DynamoDbClient yet.
evals.expected_output: A Kotlin snippet creating both a DynamoDbClient and the DynamoDBTargetSystem, plus Gradle plugin configuration.
expectations: Language is Kotlin
Creates a DynamoDbClient using its builder
Uses TODO for unresolved region values
Configures the flamingock plugin with dynamodb() module
Uses placeholders for operational values

evals.name: springboot-java-existing-client-reuse
evals.prompt: Flamingock is already in my Spring Boot app and I already have a DynamoDbClient bean. Wire DynamoDBTargetSystem without creating another client.
evals.expected_output: A Spring Boot DynamoDBTargetSystem bean that reuses the existing DynamoDbClient bean and does not generate a duplicate client.
expectations: Reuses the existing DynamoDbClient bean
Does not create a second DynamoDbClient bean
Uses YOUR_TARGET_SYSTEM_ID placeholder
Stays in the targetsystem skill boundary

evals.name: standalone-java-multiple-client-ambiguity
evals.prompt: I have two DynamoDbClient variables in my standalone Java app. Help me wire DynamoDBTargetSystem.
evals.expected_output: A response that flags the multiple-client ambiguity, asks for the intended client or shows how to choose one explicitly, without inventing a selection.
expectations: Acknowledges multiple DynamoDbClient candidates
Does not silently choose one client
Keeps the answer focused on DynamoDBTargetSystem wiring

evals.name: targetsystem-hand-off-to-change-skill
evals.prompt: My DynamoDBTargetSystem is already configured. Now create a migration to add a table.
evals.expected_output: Either a hand-off to the DynamoDB change skill or migration-oriented help that assumes the TargetSystem is already configured and does not pretend onboarding is needed.
expectations: Either redirects to flamingock-dynamodb-change or gives migration-oriented help for the requested change
Does not pretend onboarding or targetsystem setup is still needed
Keeps the response focused on migration authoring rather than redoing targetsystem wiring
